Okay, so now we're gonna move into, this is probably my favorite part. As I was going through this whole discovery phase and like looking at all these patterns and stuff, the third piece here is by far of all the stuff, like the one that I've geeked out the most about, got more excited. And I feel like it's the piece that a lot of people miss. A lot of people are good at becoming the attractive character. A lot of people are good at like causing culture building and things like that. This is where I think the majority of the mistakes are happening, especially working with inner circle members. After I become more aware of this, this is like the problem with most people's offers and most of the products. And so the third step here in how to create a mass movement is the new opportunity or the vehicle change that you're offering somebody. Okay, and so that's what we're gonna be talking about during this session. And when I started looking at everything, like you look at all of the mass movements again, this is like a big thing, like there's, and the, how do I explain this? So there's two things. There's like offers that you're trying to help people become better and there's offers where you're offering people a new opportunity, like a new thing that they can have. You look at all the mass movements and none of the mass movements were ever built upon like, hey, we're gonna help you become better at this thing. Like it's super uninspiring. And the true believer he talks about, if you look at like traditional organizations like going to college, right, or going to school, it's like you're trying to like get better and you move up through the ranks. You go to like a corporate, in the corporate world, like you start at the bottom and you're trying to improve your way to the top, which is super uninspiring. And movements are not built on that concept at all. It's not built on like, I'm gonna keep progressing it better and better and eventually I'll get somewhere. It's like, no, here's an entirely new opportunity I wanna offer you. Okay, so if you look at the spectrums of Hitler on one side and Christ at the other side, like Hitler came to the German people and what was his message? It was not like, hey guys, this whole thing sucks. All the issues we're having, like we should, you know, maybe we can get our, some of these, the fines and stuff and the fees we have to pay lower and let's change some things. Hitler came in and was like, no guys, like this whole thing is not true. I'm not a big history buff. I don't know all the details behind it. He basically came in and was like, look, the treaty that they had after World War I, like that's false. This is not true and we're gonna get rid of it. I'm gonna offer you guys a new opportunity. Like we are the German people. Like this is our thing, like this is, we're not gonna make this better for everyone. We're getting rid of this old thing that we had that's holding all of us back and we're presenting a new opportunity as people got excited and they followed him. Okay, when Christ came, he didn't come back and say, okay, you guys are delivering the love, Moses. We're gonna do a little bit better. We're gonna make a little faster. He's like, no, the love Moses has done. It's gone. It's over. We're bringing you is a new covenant, a new set, we're coming back and instead of doing animal sacrifices, we're gonna be doing a broken heart and a contrite spirit. So there's his thing and this is a new opportunity. It was different than people like, wow, like I want to be part of that. Can you look fast forward to nowadays, Steve Jobs when he came out, right? He's in his stage with his black turtleneck on. He's doing his whole thing. He didn't come to people like, hey guys, I got this thing. This is a CD player. Not gonna be able to hold six CDs. We're gonna have 26 or 36. Without him making that thing better and it would have been uninspiring, no one cared. He was like, look you guys, up to this point, you had to get CDs and all these things to be able to listen to your music. Like that's gone. Now he pulls out the iPod, you remember this? He's like, this is it. 2,500 songs done. This is a new opportunity. This is different. Every in your pocket. If you look at all the mass movements that are all built on that, offering people a new opportunity. And so what's interesting though is that most of us, we don't. We come back and we have this thing like, there's improvement and there's a new opportunity. And for whatever reason, I think it's partly because like how we've always been raised is like we're always trying to make people better. Like I'm gonna help you to be faster or stronger or smarter or better. But as soon as you have like, for someone to agree, if you say like, I'm gonna buy my course, buy my product and it's gonna make you smarter. For them to say, I want to buy that, they by default have to admit that they're dumb. If I wanna be smarter, I have to admit that I failed. If I wanna get whatever that thing is. And so there's this internal like struggle that will always happen when you're offering improvement offers. And there's a whole bunch of reasons why in the book, as you guys go through it, you'll see some of the core ones in here. Excuse me. First one is the improvement is hard, right? How many of you guys wanna come better at something? Like not mostly like it's hard. Like you've done it before like, and so like to try to do the same thing. Like I'm gonna try to do it better. Like it's just, it's painful. Like in our minds, we know like what a pain it has to go through, right? Like I'll use weight loss for example. If I've tried to lose weight 10 different times, and someone's like, hey, we're gonna lose weight together, we're gonna do it. But it's like the same thing, like we're gonna do it. Like I've tried so many times, I know what that pain looks like. I know I failed over and over and over again. So I have to like, get back and do it again. Like come back in to do it again, it's hard. Because I can remember all of the issues that happened in the past when I tried it. So that's what you're fighting against. Or if I can say like, no, like everything done in the past, like that's like it's gone. Like here's this new, again, new opportunity, new thing. Then you have no like perception of like that can be easy or gonna be hard. Like most of you guys became entrepreneurs. Like someone presented it. Like I bet half of you guys happened in a hotel room or a home party, right? And you're like, are you kidding me? Like I can get paid for this? I can tell my friends and my mom and dad and I'll get paid. Like it was this new opportunity, right? And you came in and you were so excited. But those you've done a whole bunch of times, like now it becomes like improved. How do you come better? And it's like the new opportunity is what brought you into whatever it is you're doing right now, right? Somebody offered that opportunity to you. And so, and because you didn't know yet, like I think most of you guys knew initially network marketing, how hard it was gonna be. You wouldn't have been all excited, but you don't know because it's a new thing. It's like, it's so new. Like you don't, you're not even aware of the pain you're probably gonna have to go through. Like how many of you guys, was there a lot of pain for you to be able to get to this room eventually, right? Like if somebody told me like, hey, Russell, you're gonna be an overnight success. It's gonna take you 12 years and you're not gonna sleep pretty much the entire time. It's gonna be really, really hard. Your friends, your family members are gonna leave you a lot of times. Like you're gonna go through bankruptcy almost three times. Like all this stuff, like I'm gonna sign up for that. Okay? But that's when you offer some improvement. Like they're thinking about all the stuff that failed in the past and like, I don't wanna do that again. But if I'm like, dude, this whole new thing, check this out, then they have no idea what the past can look like. And so they're more likely to take that. Does that make sense? Because improvement is hard, people's mind. Second thing is that typically people that wanna improve, like they're ambitious, right? And how many people, what would be your guess if you were to take the American population? How many of those people are actually ambitious and actually want to improve and get better? My percent. What is that? Yeah, I don't know how much, you thought I had the answer. I don't know, but it's small, right? Okay, in fact, the funny thing is, if you look at like most funnels, most value ladders, the people that end up in the very end are usually your improvement people. Like minor circles, most of you guys wanna improve your doing, right? So it's on the back end, I don't sell that on the front end. If I was like, try to convince you guys on the front, like we're gonna make your business, like it's horrible, right? So the improvement like pops out the bottom, like you'll get the people that have high ambition, but most people are not ambitious. So if you're selling improvement, you're like 99.9, whatever that percentage of the world is, it's gonna be like, I don't want that, like that sounds nice and all, but that seems way too hard, right? So we have to understand that most people do not have ambitions. The next thing is commodity pricing. When you are selling improvement, you're commodity. There's a lot of people trying to improve something, right? And so what happens when someone's a commodity, like I talked about earlier, like it drives the price down over and over and over again. So as soon as you're like, yeah, I also help people to lose weight. Oh, I also hope you like, as soon as you're in improvement, then you're grouped with everybody else trying to improve that one thing, and also in pricing drops. Or if you're a new opportunity, like I'm the only person in the world that does this, I'm the only person that can offer this, like there's no price barrier, like you can charge whatever makes sense, okay? Which is a huge thing, it's pulling that out, okay? So that's kind of the first piece to understand is every mass movement is created. The third piece of that is it has to be a new opportunity. And for a lot of you guys, this is gonna take some thinking through, like, well, what am I offering people? Like what is this new opportunity I'm giving people? I gotta plug them into some kind of, this new vehicle, this new thing. Now, as you're going through this, the next thing that I learned is from Perry Belcher, I'd never heard this before. And when he first explained to me, at first I discounted it, and I like try to talk out of it, like five or six different times, and eventually he convinced me everywhere, I was like, holy crap, he's right, I didn't even know this. And what he talked to me about was he said, that all of your decisions, and why you either do something, or why you don't do something, is tied to that magic word, status. Will this choice I'm gonna make, is it gonna increase my status, or is it going to decrease my status? That's the conversation all of your customers heads, all day long. So you're like, hey, you should get, you should get click funnels, and they're like thinking of all these things, right? Like, if I get that, and I make money, I'm gonna increase my status, that'd be awesome. What happens if I get it, I fail? My friends find out, I'm gonna decrease my status. And they're like, oh, but, they got 30 guarantees, sweet, so that means my status won't, I can get my refund if I need some, back to status normal. And they're like, oh, but then, maybe I can be in too comical, that'd be increase my status, that'd be awesome. And they're like, oh, but what if I'm not technical, I can't figure this out? And I'll say, oh, decrease, and so like going through this thing back and forth, and they're trying to figure out, because they don't want, they want the status to increase, but they don't want it to decrease. It's almost scarier for someone to have their status decrease than the potential for it to increase. In fact, that's why there's so many people, we used to call them Sibs, serial information buyers, they'll buy everything, they won't do anything. And half the reason why is because they're scared that if they do do it, and it doesn't work, then their dream disappears. And there's so much fear behind that, like, so they just don't try because they don't want to lose their dream. And so you have to understand them, you're making any kind of new opportunity to someone, is like, this is what's driving them, is this is status. Like, if I take this new opportunity, if I take this leap of faith into this new opportunity, what's that going to do to my status? Okay? And so when I first heard that, I was like, that's not true. People aren't that concerned with status. So I was asking Perry, I'm like, well, what about like my wife, for example, like, I don't like, I want to drive a Ferrari, like it increases my status. Like I feel cool behind the thing, right? My wife, like she's embarrassed of it. Like she wants like a mom car, that no one's going to worry about. And he's like, well, why did she want that? I was like, cause she doesn't want people to judge her. It's like, they judge her, what would happen? It's like, a decrease her status. Weird. So it works on both directions. It's not just like people wanting monetary things. It's also why people don't want monetary things. Like, if I do that, like when I moved into my new house, like my mom was like, if you move in that big house, then guess what's going to happen? She's like, all the people, you're going to be like those rich people on the mountain. And like all these things I'm like, mom, like, you know, like I thought I was scared, like it's going to decrease my status and buy us a nice house. And my mom was like, like I had to convince my mom that like, I'm still going to be normal. I'm still going to be a nice guy. Just, you know, and like, this is like the thing that all of us are dealing with every single day, yourselves internally, but also like everyone of your customers. Think about when you invested in inner circle. How many of you guys felt that fear of like, huh, like $25,000. Like, it's a lot of money. Okay. And you're like, I'm not going to tell anyone. Cause if I fail, I don't want them to decrease my status, right? That's like part of it. I haven't told anyone. Yeah. But as soon as you crush it with your new thing, then you're like, everyone, Oh, hell he is. I'm not as smart I am. In fact, I have a lot of friends. This is why they, they struggle. They'll get an idea. And the first thing they do, they get so excited by the idea and they call everyone. They know like, I'm doing this and they tell every single person on earth and then they're like, I'm going to go out and attempt it. And it's like, if you fail, like, you just, why'd you tell everyone already? Like, I don't tell anyone anything that's going to happen. I'm just like, try it and like, Oh crap, I failed. Good thing you know what I knew about it. Huh, you know, because I want to do Christmas. And then when he happened, you're like, yeah, you celebrate. And so it's like figuring out and we're going to talk. One big thing to say, all that's that we're doing today and tomorrow you guys is like building blocks of stuff that we're going to be plugging into our presentations later. So like there's so many ways in our presentation we're weaving status into it. So I want you guys to understand all these concepts now. And like I said, we're plugging them in tomorrow. But understanding that when you're doing your presentations, you're thinking about this, every single thing, every single thing you're offering, everything, the thing you're saying, like what's the perceived thing in their mind? Is it going to increase their status or decrease it? And if it's going to, if it's going to decrease it, what do you create to bring it back to stability? Right? Like if I'm free, if I know people, if they're going to spend $1,000 on our funnel hacks thing, if they pay money, there's immediate decrease in status that their bank account went down. So how do I, how do I bring it back to here? Okay. I offer refund period policy or whatever I think, like I'm always thinking how do we bring it back to here? And then to get them to here. So if they leave the presentation here where the only option is they've increased their status and there's in, there's no, there's no fear anymore. If I leave them in the middle that I, ah, well I could, I couldn't, or I'm down here, there's no way they're going to buy. Like I have to get them from here to here and here to here as soon as they're like, the only, like no matter what happens, it's going to increase my status. And if it doesn't get my money back, like if they believe that, then they're going to buy. Cause that's the only thing that's keeping them back. Isn't that interesting? When you understand that and how do you weave that into all these different things? In fact, a big thing that we're trying, I'm trying to do is like the stuff that we're doing is trying to make it cool. Cause when I got into this business like 12 years ago, like internet marketing stuff was not cool. Like everybody made fun of me. Like it was not a cool thing, but we're trying to make it like a cool, like a whole funnel hacker. Like all the stuff we do is like trying to make this cool because it increases their status. Like people have the shirts like I'm part of this cult shirt. Like I have all these things like trying to make it a cool thing so that it's the status symbol all the time. Reason why at Funnel Hacking Live, I have a hundred people on stage. I want them to increase their status. People see like I want to increase, it's all about status on both directions. So anyway, when I realized that I was like, oh, this is so, so exciting. So as I'm coming back and I'm making these new opportunities, I'm thinking through that. Like what's happening to their status they agree to follow me into this new thing. Okay. And not like on the religion side, like if you think about that, like when somebody, when somebody, if they're living in the world, right? And just say, they decided to become Christian. Like they're stepping into this new opportunity. Like that's a lot of fear. Sometimes I lose family members, friends. Like when you're making that step, like there's so much fear. Okay. But the same thing is happening when they're buying into what you guys are doing. Like in religion, you see like political party. Like I never told anyone like my political beliefs cause I know students that like publicly say stuff, like I have so much fear of like decrease of status. I'm just like, I'm not talking about it. Who you voting for? I don't know. Like I voted for myself. Like don't bug me. Like because of that, right? And it's the same thing. Like we see it from like, from like a really like stepping into new religion or stepping into like a huge political thing. Like we see that, but like you don't understand that your people are feeling that as well. Like for them to say, man I'm gonna spend a thousand dollars on this thing. Like they're stepping in and they're gonna have a lot of times friends or family members or people that don't understand it. And there's so much fear. So it's like, how do you, how do you create something when you bring that person in? Like they, they fill it home. Like all these other things we're talking about all kind of tie into it, but increases their status and they fill part of that. And we understand that like it just becomes, it becomes really powerful. It kind of reframes how you're, how you're doing things and how you're selling things. Okay. All right. So now I'm gonna come back here to the new opportunity. So as I was going through this, I'm like, well, how do you, like how do you make new opportunities? Like what does that look like? And I kind of figured out there's two basic, there's two basic new opportunities you can give someone. Okay. So the first one is called an opportunity switch. Okay. So right now, and we'll give this a story as well. Like the people that are, that you want to follow you, let's say they want to lose weight or they want to make money or they want to whatever that thing is like, they're already trying to get the result they want in some other vehicle, some other opportunity, right? So let's just say, I want to lose weight. So I am, I don't know, let's say I'm doing the, what's a diet? South beef. So like, I'm the South beef side. This is my world. Like this is the opportunity, the vehicle I've stepped into. So they're doing something. So you're coming and you're saying, look, your vehicle sucks. Lead pages sucks. And fusions off sucks, right? Like you're in a bad vehicle. I'm going to switch you into a new vehicle. It's going to get you to where you actually want to go. Cause you're sitting here, you're trying to get there and you're like, I can't get there. Like it's stuck. Like the stupid car doesn't work, tires are just like, this is horrible, right? So you're coming to them and saying, look, that opportunity and right now is bad. It's wrong. Whatever you, you know, fluff it up if you're nicer than me. Like this is like, come over here. This is the new opportunity. And then now we can go on, get to your result you're trying to get to, right? So it's an opportunity switch. Okay. And typically every business I believe should have one opportunity switch where I'm switching them into it is what my thing. And typically that's your front end offer. Okay. I'm taking you from whatever it is you're currently doing. I'm switching you to this new opportunity. This is the new thing I want you to go into. Okay. That's why a lot of my friends who've been doing this for 10 years, like as long as I've been doing it, the biggest thing I see them doing, and I had this problem for a while as well, is they come in and they launch new product every like 30 days and they're like switching opportunity. And then everyone buys them and they go, hey, and they switch it again. They keep switching and they keep switching. And like the customers, can you imagine like this like schizophrenia they're going through like, okay, I'm in this thing. Yeah. And they go, he's talking about this. Okay. I'm over here. And then like, and they keep moving and moving and moving. And like, because of that, the customers stop losing faith, their status goes down and they leave. They walk away. Can you notice like, what have I been talking about every single day for the last three years, you guys? Fun. Yeah, fun. I was like, I haven't changed my message. Like this is the new opportunity. Come join me. This is the new opportunity. Then when you're in, so I've switched you from whatever opportunity you're trying to, you're trying to invest in real estate, you're trying to make money doing some other things, using different, like whatever opportunity you're in, I'm switching you to my opportunity. This is where we're at. And then the rest of my value ladder is opportunity stacking. So now you're in this vehicle. How can I make this better for you? Okay. I'm going to stack an opportunity. So I'm going to say, look, using click phones, how would you like to become certified and actually make money as a click phones consultant? This is an opportunity stack. I'm not shifting your focus, but you're already in this vehicle. Here's the new opportunity. And so I'm stacking an opportunity. Now you're a certified partner. Okay. And I'm looking at things like that, certified partner. Like you're already doing funnels. How many of you guys need funnel scripts? You can actually have sales letters. Okay. I'm going to stack this opportunity. And I'm going to keep, so I'm looking at like, I'm not shifting people from my vehicle, but any other opportunity I make to make them needs to be a stack, where it's making whatever the original vehicle, they're in better. Okay. So I'm upgrading the tires, the car, like all those kinds of things. Okay. That's why I look at these kinds of things. Is the opportunity switch and the opportunity stack. And so typically, in everybody's front in business, the first thing, so it might be a book, it might be a webinar, it might be whatever. So mine's the thing that at the ad, I mean, it could be whatever that is, but you're trying to think through is like, what is the current opportunities guys are in and what am I switching them to? Right? So the first, the initial offer is a switch. Okay. And then after that, I think I have an image of this, hold on. Oh yeah, it's two, two, four, I'm gonna go. Okay. So first thing is opportunity switch. Does that make sense? So like we sell our stuff to real estate investors. We sell our stuff to stock traders because they're trying to get the same result. So the biggest thing to understand is that, the new opportunity is a vehicle, you're putting somebody in. And the goal of that is to get them some result. Right? Like that's it. And so for you guys, I want you to start thinking about that is, what's the result that these guys need? What's the result they want? And what's the vehicle I'm going to put them into? All right, who else wants to go? Carrie, whoa, nice catch. Okay, so mine is, I'm gonna be vulnerable because I don't know the answer to this. And so I'm hoping that you guys can help me define a little bit more. So mine is a fitness and exercise program, which there's a million of them out there. But the difference between mine is, is that as I've been involved in the fitness industry for over 20 years, I can give you all of my credentials with it to impress you. But the thing is, is my personal experience. And that is, I can tell you how to lose weight in your 20s of all the struggles that you have. I can tell you how to lose weight after you have babies, because I've had four of them. But the thing that's not being discussed with women is that 35 to 55 age group where you are in the thick of raising kids, you're in the thick of life. And at the same time, you wanna do something with your life. So it's just trying, and your body changes so much. So my thing is making your body great again, teaching women how to exercise less because we don't have the time. And then also eating more fat because our bodies have changed so much. It's awesome. In fact, what's crazy, in inner circle we had, we're pretty much at 100, but always going to go ebbs and flows, and anyway, I think in January we had, I don't know, like, 12 open spots, whatever, and like, they all got filled up without me doing a single ad, just cause I'm talking about it all the time, and mentioning it, and people always apply, like, is there spots, like, oh, there actually is a couple, and then, but it's just cool, like, it stays filled up without me doing anything now, at this point, but it's just kind of, it's coming from, like, just always kind of talking about that within the culture, and people just kind of ascend towards that. But again, it's a horrible front end offer, it's hard to make it work there. Chris? So I was just looking at mine, and listening to everybody else's here, I started off saying, I'm going to teach acupuncturists how to create an authentic high volume cash practice, blah, blah, blah, blah, there's, I stuff this thing with everything they could possibly want in one sentence, but, after listening to you guys, then I'm like, okay, I'm going to teach serious growth-minded acupuncturists how to successfully shift into an authentic high income, blah, blah, blah. So it's like, it's a new opportunity, it's like, where they are, their vehicle right now, they don't have a high volume, high income, time leverage practice. Things sounds good, so one of the things like, on your people, you'll find out quick, like, if that's like, resonates with them, or maybe it's, I know like a lot of times, some of my buddies are like, Cairo Gurus and Dental Gurus, is like, their shift is like, shifting from like, insurance practices to cash base. This piece is like the most important, and it's the easiest to understand, but the hardest to actually figure out. So, it may not come to you right now, but it's something that's worth working through and kind of figuring out, because when you get that, it becomes easier. Stephen had kind of an epiphany he wanted to share, which I thought was really, really cool about how to structure new opportunity for some of you guys who were really stuck, I don't know how to do that, do you wanna kind of talk about your epiphany? So, a lot of you guys, I mean, when you think about the sub market itself, obviously there's gonna be other people selling similar things to what you're doing. One of the easiest ways to create a brand new opportunity and create the niche is simply by switching up the way you deliver the same content someone else has. So, you think about what Russell was saying about that really cool, sexy offer where, hey, come out and for grand, you're gonna watch me build a business, and at the end of it, I'm gonna give it to somebody? I mean, that might be the same as what somebody else is teaching, he's merely switching up the way someone consumes it. It's a very easy, fast way to create a new opportunity. Thank you. Think about that, like, everyone else is selling books, how many books have you bought? Books don't work, you gotta come to my live event, right? Oh, everyone goes live events, it's so stupid, like, what do you do as a mastermind? Everyone is mastermind, so that can be the switch, is like the delivery mechanism, how that was really, really cool. All right, so let's come back to here. So, we talked about this, right? So, here is your customer, they're currently in some kind of opportunity, right? And they're trying to get to this goal over here they want. So, every business here, the result is different. They want something tied to their health, their relationships, their wealth, like there's something that they're trying to get, right? This is the result. And so, you're coming up to them saying, like, basically, like, this opportunity you're now sucked, you're never gonna get there no matter how hard you try, it's not your fault though, I wanna encourage your dreams, I wanna justify your failures, right? We're going through all the persuasion stuff. And you're saying, but this right here, this is the new opportunity you're jumping into, and this new opportunity is the key that's gonna get you up here, okay? And so, that's kind of what we're doing with this whole opportunity switch. Now, the problem that a lot of us as experts have, because we're so smart and we know our material and our ideas so well, then we say, okay guys, this is what we're gonna create for you. And like, we just start like giving people what we think that they want. And sometimes you get lucky and you have success because of that, but the majority of times, it's actually, it's not, the more time, majority of times you're gonna screw up what they actually want. We know they want this result, but we have to make sure we customize this in a way that they want. If you get better with the customization, the better the results are gonna be, and the more likely and more happy they're gonna be with that, okay? So, if I'm giving this new vehicle, I'm like, what do you guys want? Like, I want a CD player, like, sweet, I'm putting a CD player in that new vehicle, whatever it might be, okay? So, that's kind of the analogy. Now, to put it into practical terms, when I first got started in this business, some of my first coaches were Frank Kern and Ed Dale, they had a course called Underachievers, you guys remember that? Which was awesome, for those of you who remember it. I ended up actually buying the brand from them a couple years later, so it's so cool, I wanted to share it. And we haven't talked about it, we haven't, we shut down that whole company probably seven or eight years ago, but I love the concept. One of the core things they taught in that was this, is that there's a three step process to be successful online. And it's so simple, yet it's like the backwards. Like, most of us, we come in and we're like, okay, I have this idea for this product, you create the product, and then you're like, oh, it's done, you try to shove it down people's throats, and you try to get them to buy the thing, right? And that's, sometimes you get lucky, but most of the time, that's the issue. So, if you look at the system, it's step number one, it's find a hot market, that's what we did earlier today, making sure you're finding, here's the three markets, here's the sub market, here's our niche within there, we know that's a hot market, based on what we figured out and talked about earlier today, right? The second step then is not go create a product and sell it to them, second step is ask them what they actually want, like what do you guys actually want? Oh, you wanna do that, cool, and then third step is then give that to them, okay? So it's not find the market, create a product and shove it down their throats, it's find a market, ask them what they want, and then just give it to them. It's pretty simple, right? Guess how we came up with ClickFunnels. Every now and then our market's like, I wish we had an easy way to build funnels, all right. Hey Todd, can you build that? Yeah man, sweet, let's build that and give it to them. And that was it, right? Like it's pretty much, I mean there's little details that he had to do, I don't know what it was, but it seemed easy. But we just had to ask them what they want, so this is the key is this middle step here. Ryan the Vech, we're a really good book called Ask, and Sir Shibing has read it, and I like the principles, but for most of us, it's way intense, like it's, we had a higher team of people to do it for us, and it was awesome. That's what I'm talking about here, this is like a very simplified version of that, something that all of us can implement. In fact, all of you all are gonna implement that tonight. We're gonna build one, everyone's gonna do it, it's gonna be really fun, okay. And so that's kind of what it is. So what an Ask campaign is, is basically, when we first started doing them, they were basically landing page look like this. Okay, now a lot of you guys already have stuff happening. For those who don't yet, what in the book we talked about, this is a lot of people reading the book, aren't gonna have any kind of business up front, and so what I recommend in the book is for everyone, is initially like we need to get results for your customers first, right. So putting on a free class or free, something to get results for people first. And so the concept is like, I'm gonna put on a class, in fact one of my friends did this when he was trying to get clients. He built a class, it was called Ethical Cult Building, and it was a free class, everyone can come in, and he taught the course, then from there he got tons of clients. But he could have then gone, stuff we'll talk about tomorrow and build into a whole company, but he didn't. But the concept was he had this class out there, and he put it out there, he said, it was free, and it was like basically, he asked me a question about whatever question you have, and we're gonna take those questions, and from there we'll build the curriculum, and it was an amazing experience, I had a great time going through his course. This Ask campaign, a couple of stories just to show you guys the power in it. One of my businesses, as you know, we have a company called Overcompornography, and when we first came out with the idea for the company, we started putting it all together, and I was assuming that all of our customers would be men who were addicted to pornography, and so that's who, the product started getting created towards them, and all the marketing and everything, and by that time I heard about these Ask campaigns, and I was like, okay, I'm gonna do that, so we put it out on the page, and it was really simple, all it said is, what's your number one question about how to overcome pornography addiction? And we put it out there, we started buying, this is pre-Facebook, so we're buying Google ads to it, we were going to different parenting groups, and what was interesting, and this completely transformed the direction of the entire company, is that the people that started filling this form out were not the people who I thought I thought they were gonna be men who were struggling with pornography addiction, it was wives of men who were struggling, it was mothers that had kids that were struggling, those were the people that responded, and all the questions came in like, well, my husband's struggling, what should I do? My kid's having this issue, and all these questions started coming in, I was like, wow, the people that are actually interested in this is a whole different mark, I missed the mark completely, I was so close to it, and so it transformed everything from how we built the product, how we built this, everything changed because we figured that out, okay? And so that's like the power in these things, is like it helps you figure out exactly what your people want, and then you just give them what they ask for, and it's like the easiest way to make money, like it's the most simple thing in the world, you don't have to reinvent the wheel, it's just asking what they want. And so basically, let's see, I wanna make sure we got this, okay, so basically what we do is we're gonna ask these people, like what do you want in this vehicle, what are the things? So John's gonna come up here in a few minutes, and he's gonna be showing a couple different ways to do this, because it used to be we'd create a page like this, and we'd drive Google Ads to it, or Facebook Ads, whatever it is, and this method still works, but a lot of times nowadays there's easier, like kind of covert ways to do it that are especially in Facebook that make it really, really easy, and so John's gonna show you guys some of those things. My goal though is to get people to come through this Ask Campaign, and I'm gonna ask them that one question, and it's simple, you do have to overcomplicate it. In fact, I think, what page in the book do we have of this script? Page 38? All right, cool, I found it. So it's on this page, these are not numbered for some reason, so that's my bad. It's a page that has this image on it right here. Ken, I'll say it, and then it's on this page when you guys do find it. So basically this is the script, dear friend, or hey, my name is Russell Brunson, I know your time is valuable, so I'm gonna get right to the point, I need your advice, I need your advice, I'm putting the finishing touches on a new course called How to Make Money in Your Underwear, How to Biohack, How to Lose Weight, how to, like whatever your thing is, right, whatever your unique opportunity is, and I wanna make sure I don't leave anything out, so if you let me know, your biggest question is about how to overcome weight loss, overcome whatever, overcome dog photography addiction, how to make a potato gun, whatever your thing is, it could be anything, you may even think it's silly, all you have to do is type in your question in the box below and click submit, exchange your advice, I'll give you free access to my new course, it's happening on June 16th, this course can sell for $197 in the future, but you get special access to free to come, all you gotta do is let me know your number one question, okay, so a couple of reasons why we're doing this, the first reason, I'm gonna go back and put a slide into this, I think it's important, if you wanna take somebody to here, and you wanna give them the result, one of the biggest problems a lot of people have is they come in like, I'm gonna be an expert, I'm gonna take someone up here, I know how to do it, and they're like, I'm the greatest person in the world, they should come listen to me, I'll take you on this journey, right? But there's no proof that they actually know what they're talking about. So I got two stories to tell about this, the first one was, I have a friend, he was a high schooler at the time, his name's Alec Jetzel, and Alec wanted to get a job, this is probably five or six years ago, so during summer he went and he applied at a whole bunch of different places, everyone just basically turned him down, and so he didn't get a job, he's like, oh, I'm a talented kid, I work hard, they should give me a job, and so eventually he went down to the mall and he found like his favorite clothing store, he's like, I'm gonna get a job there, and he walked in and he was gonna apply and all that stuff, and he's just like, man, they're not gonna hire me, so he walked up to one of the managers, he's like, hey, I know this store's kinda messed up, like clothes are everywhere, do you mind? I'm just kind of like, OCD a little bit, do you mind if I just clean things up? And the manager's like, if you want, whatever, it's a high school kid sitting in a mall store, and he's like, yeah, if you wanna do my work for me, that's awesome, so Alec goes in and cleans up the things, folds the clothes, just all nice and everything, and then he comes back, hey thanks, appreciate you let me work, I'll see you later, took off. And the guys, I'm sure the people that worked there were just like, that's weird, why did that guy just do all my job for me, right? So next day Alec came back and he's like, came to the store again, he's like, hey guys, how you doing? They're like, good, he's like, hey, do you mind if I help, clean up again? Like, yeah, if you want, he's got these folding clothes, doing this whole thing, within about 20, 30 minutes, one of the manager, one of the, I don't know, manager owner, someone came out and was like, what are you doing? He's like, I just love this place, thought I'd help clean up. He's like, you want a job? He's like, yes I do, and boom, you gotta job that fast. And it came from this, I'm like, giving results first. If you look at my timeline, we did coaching for business opportunity, people like, five, I don't know, longer than that, seven, eight years ago. And then we stopped. And for about three year period of time, I went back into the business, that's when we started launching our supplement funnels, our dating funnel, like all these different businesses, because I wanted to get back into the business and we were doing it for a long time. And afterwards, after like two or three years, I was like, I want to get back into this business, like we kind of want to do the coaching stuff again. And so the first thing I didn't do was make a video and be like, hey, my name's Russell Brunson, I'm so cool, I got tons of results, I'm the most cool guy in the world, you should pay me to be in my coaching program. But how many of you guys have seen people that have that in their pitch for their coaching programs? Super annoying, I can't stand that. And I was like, I got to make sure that my stuff actually works, I want to take someone's path to get a result and then they fail, and then I'm like, man, I'm actually not any good at this. And then I was like, he would be my dream client, I would love to come in here because there's so much low hanging fruit he doesn't even know about, that'd be so cool to come in and actually help them. And then a week or two later, I was flying to San Diego and we had a friend in common, so that's my friend, he introduced me to Drew, I'd love to get to know him. Anyway, so I get there, I was like, hey, you got my book, he's like, yeah, and he kinda gives me this weird look, he's like, there's a really funny story about that, you wanna hear it? I'm like, yeah, he's like, last week, I started seeing your face pop in my newsfeed all the time. So eventually I like clicked on, I see this book, so I buy it, it's just free, shows up at my house, he said, last night I'm sitting there flipping through the book and I was like, man, how cool would it be to have Russell come and fix our funnels? And then I get a text from Carl like five minutes later, and now you're sitting in my front room, and he's like, this is kinda creepy, I was like, that is really creepy, man. And so we start talking and just kinda hanging out, and from talking with him, first off, all of us, we try to be very professional and not vulnerable and we're talking, and we're talking about business and stuff, and then he kinda got a little bit vulnerable for a second, and I was like, man, I feel like something was wrong, it wasn't like, there was something, they were struggling with someone in their business, I don't know what it was, but I was just like, is everything going okay? He's like, yeah, things are good, he's like, we're having some struggles here, and it's kinda hard to be profitable sometimes, and all this kinda stuff, he's like, but we're making over a million bucks a year, like the posturing, but I can tell something's there, and I was like, well, if you want, I gotta go back home with my kids, but I could fly out here for a day and just work with your team and kinda look at stuff and see if I can help at all if you'd be interested, and he was like, I can't really forward that right now, I was like, why don't you do it for free? And finally, after a bunch of convincing, he's like, fine, I don't know where your angle is, but if you wanna come out, whatever, so a month later, I fly out, I come to his office, and we're all sitting, in fact, if you watch the Funnel Hacker video, we're on my sketching on the white on the windows, that was his office, they didn't have whiteboards or anything, you were just in this random place, and so I'm trying to show him funnels on the mirror, or on the windows, and when I first get there though, I asked him, I'm like, I can't help you get to here, unless I know where you're really at, and so can you show me what's happening? And so they had everyone on their team, that the count was there and everyone, and the count was like, you wanna see our numbers? I was like, yeah, look at Drew, can we show him his numbers? Drew's like, yeah, let's show him our numbers, so he shows me the numbers, and I was just like, oh my gosh, I didn't realize it was, at the time they were losing about 50 grand a month, and I was like, how long has this been happening for? And it was like, almost a year, I'm like, how much longer till you're done? And he's like, we're pretty close, I'm like, oh my gosh, like, crap, okay, this went from a fun little side project to like, oh my gosh, this is a real thing, and so we went in there and we started looking at their current funnels, and there were some things that they kind of weren't doing or whatever, we talked through those, and then I was talking about, what are the new opportunities, I was a customer, I bought all your stuff, and then there was nothing else to buy, what else do you guys have? And we were thinking about this, and this, and this, and we had this idea for a supplement, it'd be a green drink, I was like, dude, that's it, and I was like, I do supplements, I'm gonna show you my supplement funnel, so I showed them my supplement funnel, I showed them the company that named my supplement, so they called them up, and eventually they came back, they got their domain name Organifi, and then they named it, and they put the other thing, and they put every last penny they had into this supplement. They were like, when it launched, it was like, if this doesn't go, we're done, like the whole company shut down, and we voxed back and forth next month or so, we helped them kind of structure out the strategy behind it, and the pricing, everything, and then they went, and they launched it, and day one it was like $20,000 in sales, day two was $30,000, day three, it kept going up from there, and in the last, about two, two and a half years since they launched, they done over $25 million with that funnel, which is crazy, right? And so what was cool was afterwards, then Drew made a video of him, and it helps that he's like, probably the best looking human on earth, but he made this video, like just telling the story about how like it wasn't even my face on anywhere, and that's where we launched The Inner Circle, and it went from one member to 10, to 10 to 15, 15 to 20, and at that point we started having someone like Liz from 20 people to 40, 40 to 60, 60 to 80, to 100, and I was like, let's stop, and we got 100 people in the Inner Circle, but it wasn't about me like pounding my own horn, like look how great I am, it was, I got someone a result, my dream client, the person I wanna work with, and I was putting that out there, and what happened is that people like Drew started applying, how many fitness people do we have in this room right now, it's insane, okay, like Drew's people started applying, Liz's people started applying, our event used to be predominantly men, now we've got like half of you are women, which is so exciting, that's because of Liz's video, and like those things we're putting out there are attracting in our dream clients, but the result is key first, like getting the result first, and then we can share that, and so the whole concept of doing the Ask Campaign this way is like, let's do a live, let's do a workshop, let's do a weekend thing, or a six hour thing, or three, or something that you're gonna get some results for people, and it's bringing a beta group of people, and let's take them through this process so you get results, I'm assuming you guys already have results, those who don't, like this is where I would start at, I said next Saturday we're doing a three hour class, I'm gonna show you guys how to go from this to this, and we're gonna go through this process together, and make it free, and you're like, I don't wanna do it for free, because then we're gonna buy in the future, it doesn't matter, like right now I gotta do it for free to get the right people in, so we run this Ask Campaign, say look, next Saturday I'm doing a free workshop, I'm gonna show you guys how to blank without blank, whatever it might be, what's your number of questions about this thing, fill out the survey, and you can come to my workshop for free next Saturday, and get a whole bunch of people in, get 50 people, get 100 people to come in, and go through this process with you, whatever it is you teach, and as you go through that process with them, then you're gonna come out the other side, and you're gonna have some amazing success stories, and those success stories become the foundation for building the rest of your business. Any business I go into right now, the first thing I'm doing is like, hey, this is a cool product, let's find some results, let's go get away free, it's 100 people if we can, and whatever it is, because those results become the sales message. People always ask me, Russell, what's the future of internet marketing? Like, the future of internet marketing is less selling, and more like getting results for people, and then showing those results. Like, that's the easiest sales video ever, is actually help someone get a result, and then just document and show that. Like, that's better than any like, copywriting course you could ever go through, you've captured them right. So that's kind of the thing. Now the way these Ask Campaigns work, and this is, even if you have an existing business, it's still, anytime you create a new offer, a new product, or new anything, or you're stagnant, or you can't figure things out, like, we use these all the time, like we're bringing them out every time we're trying to figure out the next piece. So it comes through here, and you ask them what's the number one question, and what'll happen is you start getting a whole bunch of different things coming in. So when we did this for my potato gun product initially, I started getting all of these questions coming in, right? And I ran it until I had about 100 questions. So that's kind of my goal is to get 100 people to submit questions, which means I'll have 100 people in my beta group, right? So questions start coming through, and they're all sorts of stuff, like, what kind of pipes should I use? Is the pipe's gonna blow up? Like, what's, and like, all these things start coming in, right? And after I get 100 or so, I'll stop the advertising campaign if we're paying for it. I'll say, okay, here's all the data of, like, all these people coming through. But if you look at that, like, traditionally, like, most of these things are gonna kind of fit into some groups, right? So let's say, from that, like, okay, there's, you know, 15 of these questions are all about, like, what pipes? So I'm gonna say, okay, module number one in my course is how to pick the right pipe, whatever that is, right? Okay, and the next one's maybe that are coming in, they're all about something else. So module number two is about what's the right barrel to chamber volume ratio for a potato gun, which all you guys have that mathematical formula now in the very intro of the expert seekers book, there's a little sketch with it. That is the mathematical formula, in case any of you guys are wondering what it actually is. So if you're making potato guns, now you got it, okay? So I go through here and I figure from 100 questions, I'm gonna get somewhere from six to eight modules, and that becomes the structure for my book, for my course, for my event, for whatever it is I'm trying to do. But they told me exactly what it was. They said, I wanna learn this, it's like, cool. Could I cover that in module number one, right? When we come back later, I'm gonna sell this, all these questions become like, all the sales point for when I'm selling something. Okay, if someone's like, I wanna know, what's the fastest way to get 100 people to my website, when I do my sales video, my webinar tomorrow, I'm gonna be like, okay, second thing you learn how to get 100 people, the fastest way to get 100 people to your website. Like I'm using their questions back on them, and it becomes so much easier to sell. Like they're telling you what they want, you just take that, rephrase it, and you get those people coming back in. So that's what an ask campaign is. These are something I think you guys should, everybody should be running in their business at least quarterly if you have an active business. If you're beginning, this should be like the main focus, to get people in to find out exactly what they want, because you can offer them new opportunity, but if they get in there and they're like, ah, there's no CD player in this car, then they're gonna leave, right? You offer new opportunity, they come in, they're like, oh man, CD player's there, oh, sunroof's here, how'd you know I wanted all this crap? It's because you told me, so we just gave you that. Like it's so much, and then they're in there, and then it's exactly what they want. Okay, so we're creating what they actually want, and that's what these ask campaigns are all about. Any questions about this at all? Pretty simple, pretty easy. All right, so what we're gonna do, I'm gonna do one more thing and then I'll jump back. Okay, so what we're gonna do right now is, I want you guys to figure out your question, and we're gonna kind of workshop this out real quick. The biggest thing I've seen people on mistake is like, what's your number one question about how to make money online? And when you do that, like, you get no responses or crappy ones. It's gotta be specific, like, what's your number one question about how to get more people into your funnel? What's your number one question about your hormones and how they're related to weight loss? What's your number one question? Something very specific where people are like, I actually do have a question about that. Okay, so everyone has spent two minutes kind of writing out what's your number one question about this, that'll be either the copy or for the video, for the page, the ad or the libe, but this is the thing is to figure out the question.
